implementationChanges: Updated packages/typescript/scout-n8n-node/src/nodes/KynticAiScout.node.ts to call the v1 source-system event route.
Updated packages/typescript/scout-n8n-node/README.md to document local API routing and no direct storage or Cloud writes.
architectureDecisions: Scout remains the local customer-owned Docker data plane.
Connector source-event writes route through the local Scout API where practical.
The canonical current source-event route is POST /api/v1/events/source-system?tenantSlug=<tenant>.
No compatibility server route was added for stale /api/tenants/{tenantSlug}/events/source because updating the stale package was smaller and safer.
Selector-time connector plugins remain in-process fetchers for current local demos and selector execution.
Connectors should not write directly to storage-specific internals such as Scout EF tables, LanceDB, pgvector, vector stores, relationship-set stores, or Cloud.
Cloud controls licence, entitlement, artefact, download, update, registration, heartbeat, support, and safe aggregate metadata only.
Cloud must not receive customer data, vectors, embeddings, relationship sets, attribution paths, prompts, generated customer content, evidence packs, or derived customer intelligence.

skippedProof: Docker smoke checks were not run because the local laptop policy requires opt-in environment variables for external proof paths.
Browser/Playwright proof was not run because KYNTIC_RUN_BROWSER_TESTS was not set.
Docker/PostgreSQL or external .NET proof was not run because KYNTIC_RUN_EXTERNAL_DOTNET_TESTS was not set.
Native vector, LanceDB, and pgvector proof was not run because the relevant opt-in env vars were not set and no vector-store code changed.
openRisks: Scout does not currently persist canonical exact data items, relationship sets, attribution paths, outcome events, or vectors as first-class local storage.
Selector connectors are still in-process fetchers and do not currently write canonical source records through a future richer local REST API.

SqlConnectorPlugin can directly read Scout, CustomerOps, or external PostgreSQL databases during selector execution; this is compatible with current demos but not a future durable connector write boundary.
Enterprise/Fortress live LanceDB and pgvector provider paths require explicit native-store proof and are not routine local checks.
pgvector write integration is documented as a placeholder in the audited private vector write seam.
A deterministic mapping from Scout tenant/workspace/user/source/fact IDs to Fortress postgres_pk/entity_type/layer/citation/provenance IDs is not implemented yet.
Replay or backfill through current Scout ingestion could trigger billing limits, duplicate suppression, recompute jobs, and normal audit telemetry unless a quiet local migration mode is added.
Cloud can gate entitlement and package access but must not receive raw data, vectors, evidence packs, relationship sets, attribution paths, or derived relationship intelligence.
Rollback needs concrete local scripts and proof criteria across Scout public storage, private Fortress storage, LanceDB/pgvector, and Cloud entitlement metadata.
npm install in packages/typescript/scout-n8n-node reported 2 critical audit findings in the dependency tree.
